cards.notes: Starter strike, replace when possible
Starter defend, replace when possible
Starter. Evoke front orb twice
Starter. Channel 1 lightning
Deal 18 for 2, copies itself. Slow boss scaling. Good act 1 but falls off
Zero-cost archetype has fundamental paradox problems. Draw order reliance
Deal 7 + channel lightning. Good early, falls off. Less need to rush damage in STS2
Upgraded from 4 to 5 damage per orb. Scales with enchantments. Much better in STS2
0-cost, 3 damage + 1 vulnerable. Vulnerable doesn't scale orb damage. Niche
Ancient. Was best card in STS1 but temp focus is plentiful now. No artifact to negate downside
Gain 6 block + daze in discard. Number not big enough. Status bridge doesn't work well
Innate 10 block for zero energy. Skip turn 1 decisions and set up freely. Easy A
One-time damage prevention. Not as crazy without Heart but still solid
Lose 1 orb slot, gain 2 strength + 2 dex. Good early but too slow once doing orb stuff
One copy solves endgame output. Orb slots + focus is multiplicative
Channel 1 random orb. Needs buffs. Almost never pick it
Premium block common. 7 block + 1 energy next turn. Never falls off
Multi-enemy fights it blocks a ton for zero energy. Upgrade removes exhaust. Disgusting card
Meme card. Number too low. Zero-cost archetype problems
Deal 6 + channel frost. Reads 'deal 6, block 2 for rest of fight.' Excellent common
Gain 6 block + transform statuses to fuel. Number not great but effect is strong
Deal 7, draw per unique orb. More orb types in STS2. Usually draws 2
2 energy power, channel 2 dark + evoke leftmost each turn. Awkward, doesn't enter deck often
Power giving block per unique orb each turn. Passive scaling that never falls off
Best common. Frost + draw. Upgrade to draw 2 is insane. Main enabler for defect
Boss scaling that solves endgame. Random powers are strong. More time to set up in STS2
Darkness+ baseline from STS1. Insane single-target damage. Upgrade triggers dark passive twice
Straight into S. One permanent focus outscales almost every fight. Upgrade to 2 is nuts
Cheat energy. Really nice with Coolheaded. Good for power setup turns
Probably the best card in the class. Playing best card twice is broken. Worth carrying as brick
Multiplayer card, not relevant in solo
2 energy power returning zero-cost attacks. Bad Echo Form. Output not enough
Gain 13 block + 2 wounds. Solid early block but wounds brick future draw cycles
Exhaust all statuses, deal 8 damage per. Status package needs more support
Common. Deal 9 + 1 temp focus. Upgrade: 11 + 2 focus. De facto common that never falls off
Zero energy, deal 5, draw 1 if <3 cards played. Free damage. Don't take with Echo Form
Channel plasma for 2 energy. Less needed. Better/easier ways to make energy
Scales into insane block over the game. Combos with Echo Form. Rare so comes pre-upgraded
Nerfed 1 block from STS1, doesn't matter. Makes two frost, that's all you need to know
Gain 5 block, channel glass orb. New orb type for AoE. Very easy to pick in act 1
Zero energy, deal 3 + apply weak. Overperforming card. Standalone good
Deal 4x3 + add slab. Number too low vs ball lightning. Payoff not good enough
Power: end of turn deal 6 AoE if you have frost. Condition basically always met. Doesn't fall off
Zero energy, 3 damage per energy spent. Usually does 9. As a rare, numbers don't justify slot
Put card from discard to hand. Not as broken as people think. Could be top of B
Best temp focus card. Zero energy, gain 2 focus this turn (upgrades to 3). Common rarity. Bonkers
Deal 26 AoE, lose 3 focus. Better in STS2 but still a break in hard fights
3 energy, deal 19 + channel 3 frost. Slightly overcosted but frost is just that good
Multiplayer card, not relevant in solo
First status drawn each turn draws 2. Average card, saves draw cycles from bricking
Gain 9 block for 1 energy. Not as good as Charge Battery but big number early
Slow block + channel lightning. Does damage while blocking in act 1
Trigger rightmost orb passive at start of turn. Worse than STS1 without permanent focus but still great
Draw 1 extra per turn. Slightly better than STS1 due to needing to line up Hot Fix
Was easy S in STS1 but payoff for playing it is worse. Can still go infinite with coolheaded
Free orb slot, replaces itself, scales throughout the fight. No downside
Deal 10, reduces to zero cost. Decent early common, nothing exceptional
X energy evoke rightmost X times. Less need for mega dark orb. Energy harder to combine
Deal 10, apply 2 weak, channel dark. Weakness + dark orb. Very solid
Draw 2 + burn. Burn bricks future cycles which matters more now with temp focus
Ancient. Evoke rightmost orb 4 times. Block 20 from frost evoke or one-shot boss with dark
2 energy channel 1 of each orb type. Jack of all trades, does nothing well
Shuffle all cards, draw 4, exhaust. Not doing anything crazy as a rare but has uses
3 energy, deal 9x2 + channel 2 glass. Clunky, no energy left to block
Deal 13 draw 1; goes to zero cost when status created. Combos with turbo
Exhaust a card, gain 2 energy next turn. Worse than STS1 recycle (delayed energy)
Deal 7 + draw 4 but discard non-zero. Paradox: can't click first, can't build toward it
2 energy, 11 block + channel dark. Keeps getting bumped up. 11 block is solid for setup character
Deal 11 AoE + evoke all orbs. Evoking is often upside in STS2
Next power played twice, exhaust. Too situational. Less defrags to double
Draw 3 for 1 energy. Cares about drawing powers and hot fixes. Very clickable
Deal 5 AoE per status created. How often do you create statuses? Situational
Channel glass each turn. Early game damage. Not exceptional but fills a role
Channel lightning per power played. No heat sinks makes it worse. Situational
Gain 1 energy per power played. Less power spam in STS2 so less needed
Deal 24; if kills, gain 3 energy. Pseudo-AoE. Good in hallways, falls off vs bosses
Zero energy, gain 4 energy, exhaust. Tons of acceleration. Lets you chain draw and play all powers
Deal 6 AoE + draw 1. AoE more needed in STS2. Self-cycles. Just fine
Can make 4-8 focus per turn based on unique orbs. Upgrade removes exhaust. Loop for insane scaling
Deal 12, next power costs zero. Doesn't cheat much energy. Number too low
X cost channel X lightning. Less energy, less focus. Better ways to deal boss damage
Zero energy, deal 3 + trigger all lightning. Solid early damage, scales with focus
Extra 6 damage per lightning evoke. Awkward to pick. Harder to evoke than let sit
Channel random orb per status created. Status archetype too weak currently
Zero energy, gain 2 energy + void in discard. Bridges into status cards. Energy gen is great
Deal 5x2 + play random attack from draw. Weird, random cards not desirable
Channel lightning equal to lightning already channeled. Exponential scaling for boss damage
Random power into hand, exhaust. Desperation pick only. Average power quality too low
